page: Microsoft Azure DC64ads_cc_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DCADCCV5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical AMD EPYC 7763 cores, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The x86_64 architecture supports hardware-assisted virtualization via AMD SVM, alongside cryptographic acceleration instructions such as AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. For local storage, the server includes a 2458 GB local disk and is Premium Storage capable, facilitating high-capacity data handling without requiring external attachments. No GPUs are present on this configuration, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate highly capable multi-threaded processing and memory subsystems. The server achieves a top-tier rating in PassMark memory performance and strong top-25% results in multi-core stress-ng, PassMark CPU, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Cryptographic operations and database tasks are highly efficient, as evidenced by strong scores in PassMark CPU encryption and Redis SET operations, which reached 63.02 million operations per second. However, large-block uncached memory bandwidth and single-threaded tasks, such as gzip compression, perform in the average tier.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and substantial local storage, this server is well-suited for database operations, memory-intensive general-purpose computing, and multi-threaded data processing. While it handles small-model LLM inference efficiently, it exhibits weak performance on large-model text generation, making it less suitable for heavy generative AI workloads without dedicated GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure DC64ads_cc_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DCADCCV5 family, powered by 64 dedicated vCPUs on a 32-core AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of RAM and a 2458 GB local disk with Premium Storage capabilities. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server delivers strong multi-core performance, highlighted by top-tier PassMark memory scores and strong Redis SET operations. While single-threaded tasks and large-model LLM text generation represent performance tradeoffs, its balanced resource profile makes it highly efficient for database workloads, multi-threaded data processing, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC64ads_cc_v5 is a general-purpose server featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 2458 GB local disk, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DC64ads_cc_v5 features 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 2458 GB local disk for high-performance database and general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced sixty-four vCPU virtual machine featuring high-speed AMD EPYC processing, substantial local storage, and top-tier memory performance.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7763 processors at 3.525 GHz
256 GB of system memory paired with a 2458 GB local disk
Top-tier memory performance and strong multi-core processing benchmarks
Optimized for database operations, Redis, and multi-threaded workloads
Lacks GPU acceleration, resulting in lower efficiency for large LLM inference
